# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

require 'sendgrid-ruby'

class SendGridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  include SendGrid

  default from: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_DEFAULT_FROM', 'noreply@example.com')

  def initialize
    super
    @sendgrid_api_key = ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_API_KEY', nil)
  end

  def welcome_email(user)
    @user = user
    @url = root_url

    if use_sendgrid?
      send_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Welcome to Our Application',
        template_id: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_WELCOME_TEMPLATE_ID', nil),
        dynamic_template_data: {
          user_name: @user.name,
          login_url: @url
        }
      )
    else
      mail(to: @user.email, subject: 'Welcome to Our Application')
    end
  end

  def password_reset_email(user, reset_token)
    @user = user
    @reset_token = reset_token
    @reset_url = edit_password_reset_url(reset_token)

    if use_sendgrid?
      send_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Password Reset Instructions',
        template_id: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_PASSWORD_RESET_TEMPLATE_ID', nil),
        dynamic_template_data: {
          user_name: @user.name,
          reset_url: @reset_url
        }
      )
    else
      mail(to: @user.email, subject: 'Password Reset Instructions')
    end
  end

  def notification_email(user, subject:, body:, attachments_list: [])
    @user = user
    @body = body

    if use_sendgrid?
      send_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: subject,
        content_body: body,
        attachments_list: attachments_list
      )
    else
      attachments_list.each do |attachment|
        attachments[attachment[:filename]] = {
          mime_type: attachment[:type],
          content: attachment[:content]
        }
      end
      mail(to: @user.email, subject: subject)
    end
  end

  def bulk_email(recipients, subject:, body:, template_id: nil)
    if use_sendgrid?
      send_bulk_via_sendgrid(
        recipients: recipients,
        subject: subject,
        content_body: body,
        template_id: template_id
      )
    else
      recipients.each do |recipient|
        mail(to: recipient[:email], subject: subject) do |format|
          format.html { render html: body.html_safe }
          format.text { render plain: body }
        end
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def use_sendgrid?
    @sendgrid_api_key.present? && !Rails.env.test?
  end

  def sendgrid_client
    @sendgrid_client ||= SendGrid::API.new(api_key: @sendgrid_api_key)
  end

  def build_sendgrid_mail(options = {})
    mail_object = SendGrid::Mail.new

    from_email = options.fetch(:from, default_params[:from])
    mail_object.from = SendGrid::Email.new(email: from_email)

    personalization = SendGrid::Personalization.new
    personalization.add_to(SendGrid::Email.new(email: options[:to]))

    if options[:cc]
      Array(options[:cc]).each do |cc_email|
        personalization.add_cc(SendGrid::Email.new(email: cc_email))
      end
    end

    if options[:bcc]
      Array(options[:bcc]).each do |bcc_email|
        personalization.add_bcc(SendGrid::Email.new(email: bcc_email))
      end
    end

    personalization.subject = options[:subject]

    if options[:dynamic_template_data]
      options[:dynamic_template_data].each do |key, value|
        personalization.add_dynamic_template_data(key.to_s => value)
      end
    end

    mail_object.add_personalization(personalization)

    if options[:template_id]
      mail_object.template_id = options[:template_id]
    elsif options[:content_body]
      mail_object.add_content(SendGrid::Content.new(
        type: 'text/html',
        value: options[:content_body]
      ))
    end

    if options[:attachments_list]
      options[:attachments_list].each do |attachment_data|
        attachment = SendGrid::Attachment.new
        attachment.content = Base64.strict_encode64(attachment_data[:content])
        attachment.type = attachment_data[:type] || 'application/octet-stream'
        attachment.filename = attachment_data[:filename]
        attachment.disposition = attachment_data[:disposition] || 'attachment'
        mail_object.add_attachment(attachment)
      end
    end

    if options[:categories]
      Array(options[:categories]).each do |category_name|
        category = SendGrid::Category.new(name: category_name)
        mail_object.add_category(category)
      end
    end

    mail_object.add_category(SendGrid::Category.new(name: Rails.env))

    tracking_settings = SendGrid::TrackingSettings.new
    click_tracking = SendGrid::ClickTracking.new(enable: true, enable_text: false)
    tracking_settings.click_tracking = click_tracking
    open_tracking = SendGrid::OpenTracking.new(enable: true)
    tracking_settings.open_tracking = open_tracking
    mail_object.tracking_settings = tracking_settings

    mail_object
  end

  def send_via_sendgrid(options = {})
    mail_object = build_sendgrid_mail(options)
    response = sendgrid_client.client.mail._('send').post(request_body: mail_object.to_json)

    handle_sendgrid_response(response, options[:to])
  rescue StandardError => e
    handle_sendgrid_error(e, options[:to])
  end

  def send_bulk_via_sendgrid(options = {})
    mail_object = SendGrid::Mail.new

    from_email = options.fetch(:from, default_params[:from])
    mail_object.from = SendGrid::Email.new(email: from_email)

    options[:recipients].each do |recipient|
      personalization = SendGrid::Personalization.new
      personalization.add_to(SendGrid::Email.new(email: recipient[:email]))
      personalization.subject = options[:subject]

      if recipient[:dynamic_data]
        recipient[:dynamic_data].each do |key, value|
          personalization.add_dynamic_template_data(key.to_s => value)
        end
      end

      mail_object.add_personalization(personalization)
    end

    if options[:template_id]
      mail_object.template_id = options[:template_id]
    elsif options[:content_body]
      mail_object.add_content(SendGrid::Content.new(
        type: 'text/html',
        value: options[:content_body]
      ))
    end

    mail_object.add_category(SendGrid::Category.new(name: Rails.env))
    mail_object.add_category(SendGrid::Category.new(name: 'bulk'))

    response = sendgrid_client.client.mail._('send').post(request_body: mail_object.to_json)
    handle_sendgrid_response(response, options[:recipients].map { |r| r[:email] }.join(', '))
  rescue StandardError => e
    handle_sendgrid_error(e, 'bulk recipients')
  end

  def handle_sendgrid_response(response, recipient)
    status = response.status_code.to_i

    if status >= 200 && status < 300
      Rails.logger.info("[SendGridMailer] Email successfully sent to #{recipient}. Status: #{status}")
      true
    else
      Rails.logger.error(
        "[SendGridMailer] Failed to send email to #{recipient}. " \
        "Status: #{status}, Body: #{response.body}, Headers: #{response.headers}"
      )

      if Rails.env.production?
        raise SendGridDeliveryError.new(
          "SendGrid delivery failed with status #{status}: #{response.body}",
          status,
          response.body
        )
      end

      false
    end
  end

  def handle_sendgrid_error(error, recipient)
    Rails.logger.error(
      "[SendGridMailer] Exception sending email to #{recipient}: " \
      "#{error.class} - #{error.message}\n#{error.backtrace&.first(5)&.join("\n")}"
    )

    raise error if Rails.env.production?

    false
  end

  class SendGridDeliveryError < StandardError
    attr_reader :status_code, :response_body

    def initialize(message, status_code = nil, response_body = nil)
      @status_code = status_code
      @response_body = response_body
      super(message)
    end
  end
end
